response.justification: Strong individual-contributor profile with broad full-stack and developer-tooling exposure (test runners, CI, MCP code generators, custom web server), but the resume shows no formal people-management experience, which is the core requirement for a Level II Manager role. Technical leadership is implied by Staff/Senior titles, but there is no evidence of direct reports, 1:1s, performance management, or organizational-change leadership. Mobile-platform depth is also thin — a brief iOS/Apple TV stint in 2015-2016 is the only relevant exposure, well short of what a mobile-ecosystem productivity org expects.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot engineering with hands-on dev-tooling work (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD, MCP tooling)
Demonstrated technical mentorship via workshops, dojos, and a multi-module Selenium course
Comfortable across the stack and across languages, useful for guiding tool/system design
concerns: No explicit people-management experience (no direct reports, 1:1s, or hiring/perf cycles described)
Minimal mobile-platform background — only ~1 year of iOS/tvOS work nearly a decade ago
Currently on a career gap and most recent role was 6 months, raising fit/availability questions for a leadership hire
